post.subject: What about blogging
post.body: I’ve just spent the last 30mins or so reading some articles I printed yesterday on BBC News relating to Google and Blogging. It appears that Blogging is... well, huge. Yeah in case you didn’t already know that, its fuckin massive. Blogger had 200,000 users last year, yup a full year ago, before Google took over! Now I cant imagine how many millions of people use Blogger along. Livejournal say they have 3,082,448 subscribers, of which 1,482,196 are described as active. And there are so many more blogging services and tools out there! Me on the other hand, I’m not trying to get publicity, fame or to be noticed. I just feel its time to put some stuff on the web. In my case a lot of pictures, because I love taking pictures of the world around me and the people with me. I realised this about a year ago really. That pictures must be taken of things that happen to us or else we will just forget these things happened. I’m not going to go mad on the self-indulgent, self-opinionated drivel that comes from a lot of blogs. I want mine to be a place where I can share the out of the ordinary, bizarre, slightly interesting, accounts of my days. Maybe the odd rant, the odd bit of opinion (well I’m not going to be writing much of other people’s opinions), the occasional boring stuff… but I will keep that to a minimum. So basically, I don’t care if you read this or not. I’m not writing this for the masses, nor am I doing it so I can get more ‘virtual friends’, I’m simply doing it as a way of recording my life, through pictures and words. If you are some random stranger living in a distant land, or round the corner, that’s fine by me… just don’t come looking for me cos you were slightly offended by anything I may say! Google revamps blogging service (BBC News) A Blog For Everyone (BBC News) All over for blogs? (BBC News) Fame or misfortune beckons for weblogs? (BBC News) I blog, therefore I am (BBC News)
